issues.current_text: this lazy list, with `f` applied to each element as it is evaluated
issues.problem: `tapEach` is implemented as `map { a => f(a); a }`, which returns a new `LazyListIterable`, not `this`. Saying "this lazy list" suggests the same object is returned, which is technically inaccurate.
issues.suggestion: a new lazy list with the same elements as this lazy list, with `f` applied to each element as it is evaluated

issues.current_text: a new lazy list identical to this one except that the element at position `index` is `elem`; throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException` if `index` is negative or beyond the end of this lazy list
issues.problem: The @return tag should describe what is returned. Exception behavior is conventionally documented via @throws (or in the main description). Mixing them in @return is a minor style inconsistency.
issues.suggestion: a new lazy list identical to this one except that the element at position `index` is `elem`

issues.current_text: a new lazy list whose head is `hd` and whose tail is `tl`, with both already evaluated
issues.problem: Saying the tail is "already evaluated" is misleading: `tl` is itself a `LazyListIterable` whose contents may not be forced. What's eager is the cons cell structure (head/tail are stored directly rather than behind a thunk).
issues.suggestion: a new lazy list whose head is `hd` and whose tail is `tl`, with the cons cell constructed eagerly (no thunk wrapping `hd` or `tl`)

issues.current_text: a new lazy list of pairs whose length is the maximum of the lengths of this lazy list and `that`, padding the shorter side with `thisElem` or `thatElem` as appropriate
issues.problem: "As appropriate" is vague; readers benefit from being told explicitly which element pads which side, even though the @param descriptions cover it.
issues.suggestion: a new lazy list of pairs whose length is the maximum of the lengths of this lazy list and `that`, padding this lazy list with `thisElem` or `that` with `thatElem` when one is shorter

issues.current_text: a lazy list consisting of the first `n` elements of this lazy list, or all elements if there are fewer than `n`
issues.problem: Does not mention behavior when `n <= 0`. The implementation returns empty in that case. Minor completeness issue.
issues.suggestion: a lazy list consisting of the first `n` elements of this lazy list, or all elements if there are fewer than `n`; empty if `n <= 0`

general_feedback: The @return documentation that was filled in is accurate, clear, and stylistically consistent with the existing @param/@tparam tags. Phrasing reliably starts lowercase, uses backticks for code references, and matches the surrounding Scaladoc voice. A few entries could be tightened: `tapEach` should not claim it returns `this`; `updated` mixes throw behavior into @return; `eagerCons` overstates how "evaluated" the tail is; and a couple of return descriptions could be more explicit about boundary behavior (`take` with non-positive `n`, `zipAll` padding direction). No errors were introduced — these are warnings/suggestions.
